violations.text: Raw animal foods (fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (ribs) in the 1-door lowboy on the cookline. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated ribs to another refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (2 tomato cutters, 1 potato cutter, and two knives in knife holder) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Staff relocated above utensils/equipment to dishwasher to be washed, rinsed and sanitized at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The following equipment was observed not in good repair: -can opener blade located in expo station -freezer door gasket located in kitchen -2 door refrigerator located in cream room -cold water flow on handwashing sink located in prep room -left knob on handwashing located in bathroom PIC informed and will change blade out and will place service order for gasket. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
Observed several non-food contact surfaces throughout the establishment of han accumulation of dust, dirt, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Observed hoses connected to the faucet in the (bakery sink). This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. PIC informed and will correct. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Observed the ice machine lacking an airgap located in basement. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

The floor and wall junctures throughout the kitchen and prep areas do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. The floors throughout kitchen, prep, ware washing, hallway, walk-in freezer, and storage areas are in poor repair. Physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Observed food debris and residue beneath the equipment, cook-line and various shelves throughout the establishment. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the cream room. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage or employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
The floors throughout the kitchen, prep, ware-washing, and storage areas are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

Several foods (see temperatures) were observed held at 46-56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, food had been in prep unit for less than four hours. All food was removed from prep unit and placed in walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings observed throughout the facility and live activity noted during inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024

A flip top prep unit was observed in poor repair (not holding adequate ambient temperature). All food was removed and unit was taken out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The floors throughout the kitchen, prep, ware-washing, and storage areas are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

Sliced tomato, mayonnaise, sliced turkey, and pasta were observed held at 46-58 degrees Fahrenheit. Inspector determined that food had been prepared less than four hours prior to storage and did not require disposal. Food was moved to walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) lobster was observed thawing in walk-in cooler without breaking seal and/or removing from packaging to allow for adequate oxygen transfer rate. Due to lobster being 35 degrees at time of inspection, a disposal was not needed. Lobster was sliced open at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings observed throughout the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024

Doors to the outside, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in several refrigeration units have an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The condensate from a refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The floors throughout kitchen, prep, ware-washing, and storage areas are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the food storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024
